cape_question.content:
Yes I can do that — let's start with the cape.

Which of these does your Jawa have?

1 Vinyl cape - smooth plastic
2 Cloth cloak - fabric
3 Or neither. Just a naked figure

Just reply with 1, 2 or 3.

vinyl_warning.content:
Ok — let’s treat this as a potential vinyl cape Jawa, not confirmed yet.

Original vinyl cape Jawas are very valuable, so unfortunately there are many fakes and cut-down Ben Kenobi capes around.

This app is a useful starting point, but don’t rely on it alone. For anything valuable, get confirmation from experienced collector groups as well.

First, compare your cape shape, surface texture and colour against this reference:

images.title: Jawa Vinyl Cape Authentication Reference
images.caption:
Compare:
• cape shape
• surface texture on both sides
• colour compared with an original Ben cape
• signs of a cut-down cape

A cut-down Ben cape is usually noticeably darker and the shape will not match correctly.

vinyl_more_info.content:
For more detailed information and reference photos, check:
https://www.variantvillain.com/accessory-guide/jawa-vinyl-cape/

cloth_reply.content:
Great, so you have a cloth cloaked Jawa.

Let’s check the figure markings first to see which version you have. Once we’ve narrowed down the figure, we can check whether the cloak and blaster look right as well.

naked_reply.content:
Right — so your Jawa is missing its cape, what collectors often refer to as a naked figure.

In that case, let’s identify the figure itself.

leg_marking_question.content:
Next step:
Check the Country of Origin markings on the legs.

Reply with:

1 for Hong Kong
2 if it doesn't show Hong Kong
3 if you can't tell / it doesn't appear to have either

no_coo_result.content:
That points to the Kader China M2 No COO Jawa.

It should show just:
© G.M.F.G.I. 1977

No HONG KONG underneath.

G.M.F.G.I. stands for General Mills Fun Group Incorporated, with 1977 being the year the figure was originally licensed.

Compare your leg marking with this reference:

images.title: Kader China M2 No COO
images.caption:
Kader China M2 2a and M2 2b:
Only © G.M.F.G.I. 1977. No HONG KONG marking.

Notice the size difference between 2a and 2b, and how the 2a version’s text aligns with the fold.

no_coo_more.content:
This variant was originally paired with:
• an M2 Kader Jawa Blaster - short rear bump
• a small hood, smooth cloth cloak

Important:
Don’t rely on just the COO. Mould, paint colour, plastic colour and figure assembly traits are also needed to confirm your figure’s origins.

Full Jawa guide on Variant Villain:
https://www.variantvillain.com/characters/sw/jawa/#f2

unclear_marking.content:
If it doesn’t show either expected marking, be careful.

For original vintage Jawa figures, the normal left-leg markings are either:

• © G.M.F.G.I. 1977 with HONG KONG underneath
• or just © G.M.F.G.I. 1977 with no HONG KONG underneath

Possible explanations include:
• very damaged, worn or faint markings
• poor lighting / difficult angle
• bootleg
• reproduction
• Retro Collection
• modern figure

For now, check under strong light and look again at the back of the left leg.

hong_kong_refs.content:
Ok, compare your left-leg marking with these reference images and choose the closest match.

Use the following images to compare your leg marks. On the images, look at the parts indicated in green.

images.title: 1. Kader M1
images.caption:
Kader M1 1a:
The copyright C (©) aligns with the G of HONG, and the F of G.M.F.G.I. aligns with the G of KONG.

Kader M1 1b:
The copyright C (©) aligns with the O of HONG, and the second G of G.M.F.G.I. aligns with the G of KONG.

images.title: 2. Kader M2
images.caption:
Kader M2 1a:
The right side of the copyright C (©) aligns with the H of HONG, and the 1 of 1977 aligns with the G of KONG.

Kader M2 1b:
The right side of the copyright C (©) aligns with the H of HONG, and the middle of the 9 and 7 of 1977 aligns with the G of KONG.

images.title: 3. Unitoy M3
images.caption:
Unitoy M3 1a:
The first G of G.M.F.G.I. aligns with the H of HONG, and the middle of the 77 from 1977 aligns with the G of KONG.

Unitoy M3 1b:
The first G of G.M.F.G.I. aligns with the H of HONG, and the middle of the 77 from 1977 aligns with the G of KONG.

images.title: 4. Unitoy / Lili Ledy M4
images.caption:
Unitoy / Lili Ledy M4 1a:
The M of G.M.F.G.I. aligns with the H of HONG, and the middle of the second 7 from 1977 aligns with the G of KONG.

Unitoy / Lili Ledy M4 1b:
The M of G.M.F.G.I. aligns with the H of HONG, and the middle of the second 7 from 1977 aligns with the G of KONG.

choose_hong_kong_ref.content:
Full Jawa guide on Variant Villain:
https://www.variantvillain.com/characters/sw/jawa/

If you can match your figure to one of the above reference images, please reply with:

1 for Kader M1
2 for Kader M2
3 for Unitoy M3
4 for Unitoy / Lili Ledy M4

result_kader_m1.content:
Closest match: Kader M1.

For a more detailed look at the Kader M1 Jawas, visit:
https://www.variantvillain.com/characters/sw/jawa/#f1

result_kader_m2.content:
Closest match: Kader M2.

For a more detailed look at the Kader M2 Jawas, visit:
https://www.variantvillain.com/characters/sw/jawa/#f2

result_unitoy_m3.content:
Closest match: Unitoy M3.

For a more detailed look at the Unitoy M3 Jawas, visit:
https://www.variantvillain.com/characters/sw/jawa/#f3

result_unitoy_lili_ledy_m4.content:
Closest match: Unitoy / Lili Ledy M4.

For a more detailed look at the Unitoy / Lili Ledy M4 Jawas, visit:
https://www.variantvillain.com/characters/sw/jawa/#f3

options.label: Yes, help me identify the blaster

done.content: No problem. Ask me anytime if you want help with another vintage Star Wars figure, COO, cloak, cape or accessory.
